name: LLM Analyzer
summary: Passes the incoming payload through an LLM with a configurable prompt and emits the analysis result.
description:
# LLM Analyzer

Sends the incoming payload to a large language model along with a user-defined prompt. The model's response is emitted as an `AnalysisResult` event. Supports structured JSON output mode for downstream parsing.

## Use cases
- Classify or score incoming documents
- Extract structured data from free-form text
- Summarize long content into short descriptions
- Detect sentiment or intent

## Notes
- The full payload is serialized to JSON and injected into the prompt context
- Use `outputFormat: json` when the next node expects a structured object
- Token usage is reported in `tokensUsed` for cost monitoring

inputs.name: AnyPayload
inputs.description: Accepts any JSON payload. The full object is serialized and provided as context to the LLM.

outputs.name: AnalysisResult
outputs.description: Emitted after the LLM responds with the analysis.

result.description: The LLM's response. String when outputFormat is 'text', object when 'json'.

model.description: The model identifier that produced this result.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Instruction sent to the LLM along with the payload. Use {{field}} to reference payload fields.

x-ui.placeholder: Analyze the input and extract the key topics as a JSON array.

model.title: Model
model.description: LLM model to use for analysis.

temperature.title: Temperature
temperature.description: Sampling temperature. Lower values produce more deterministic output.

maxTokens.title: Max Tokens
maxTokens.description: Maximum number of tokens in the model's response.

outputFormat.title: Output Format
outputFormat.description: Whether the model should respond as plain text or structured JSON.

systemPrompt.title: System Prompt
systemPrompt.description: Optional system-level instruction that sets the model's persona or constraints.

x-ui.placeholder: You are a helpful data extraction assistant.
